documents.name: Functional mock Solicitation.docx
documents.text:
Document 1 — Mock Government Solicitation/RFP
Filename: Mock_FAR_DFARS_Cybersecurity_RFP.docx
REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP)
Solicitation Number:
DOD-CYBER-2026-009
Title:
Enterprise Cybersecurity Operations and Information Assurance Support Services
Issuing Agency:
Department of Defense
Defense Information Technology Contracting Office
Contract Type:
Firm Fixed Price / Time and Materials Hybrid
Proposal Due Date:
September 30, 2026
SECTION C — DESCRIPTION/SPECIFICATIONS
C.1 Background
The Department of Defense (DoD) requires contractor support for cybersecurity operations, continuous monitoring, vulnerability management, incident response, and protection of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI).
The contractor shall perform all work in accordance with applicable Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) requirements.
C.2 Scope of Work
The contractor shall provide:
cybersecurity monitoring,
vulnerability assessments,
incident response,
protection of government information systems,
compliance reporting.
C.3 Security Assessment Requirements
The contractor shall perform an initial cybersecurity assessment within 90 calendar days after contract award.
The assessment shall include:
vulnerability identification,
risk analysis,
remediation recommendations,
compliance assessment.
C.4 Continuous Monitoring Requirements
The contractor shall provide continuous monitoring services.
The contractor shall:
monitor network activity 24 hours per day, 7 days per week,
detect unauthorized access attempts,
maintain security event logs,
provide monthly cybersecurity reports.
SECTION I — CONTRACT CLAUSES
FAR 52.204-21
Basic Safeguarding of Covered Contractor Information Systems
The contractor shall apply minimum safeguarding requirements to protect covered contractor information systems.
The contractor shall:
limit system access,
identify authorized users,
protect information systems from unauthorized access,
report cybersecurity incidents.
FAR 52.204-25
Prohibition on Contracting for Certain Telecommunications and Video Surveillance Services or Equipment
The contractor shall not provide or use prohibited telecommunications equipment or services identified under this clause.
The contractor shall certify compliance with this requirement.
FAR 52.222-50
Combating Trafficking in Persons
The contractor shall comply with all requirements regarding prevention of trafficking in persons.
The contractor shall:
maintain appropriate policies,
notify the Government of violations,
provide employee awareness training.
FAR 52.245-1
Government Property
The contractor shall maintain adequate property management systems for Government-furnished property.
The contractor shall:
maintain property records,
safeguard government property,
report loss or damage.
DFARS CLAUSES
DFARS 252.204-7012
Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
The contractor shall:
implement adequate security measures,
protect Covered Defense Information,
report cyber incidents within 72 hours,
preserve affected systems and data.
DFARS 252.204-7019
Notice of NIST SP 800-171 DoD Assessment Requirements
The contractor shall maintain compliance with NIST SP 800-171 requirements.
The contractor shall provide evidence of assessment scores upon request.
DFARS 252.204-7020
NIST SP 800-171 DoD Assessment Requirements
The contractor shall:
complete required cybersecurity assessments,
maintain current assessment information,
provide assessment results to the Government.
DFARS 252.239-7017
Notice of Supply Chain Risk
The contractor shall disclose supply chain risks associated with information technology products and services.
SECTION L — PROPOSAL INSTRUCTIONS
The offeror shall provide:
Technical approach.
Cybersecurity compliance approach.
FAR/DFARS clause compliance matrix.
Staffing plan.
Past performance.
Risk management plan.
Cost proposal.
SECTION M — EVALUATION FACTORS
Technical Capability — 40%
Cybersecurity Compliance — 30%
Past Performance — 20%
Cost — 10%
END OF SOLICITATION

documents.name: Functional mock Proposal.docx
documents.text:
Document 2 — Contractor Proposal
Filename: Acme_Defense_Cybersecurity_Proposal.docx
TECHNICAL PROPOSAL
Enterprise Cybersecurity Operations Support Services
Submitted By:
Acme Defense Solutions LLC
1. Technical Approach
Acme Defense Solutions will provide cybersecurity operations support through:
continuous monitoring,
vulnerability assessments,
incident response,
cybersecurity reporting.
2. Cybersecurity Assessment Approach
Acme will perform an initial cybersecurity assessment following contract award.
The assessment will include:
vulnerability identification,
security risk analysis,
remediation recommendations.
The assessment will be completed within 120 days after contract award.
3. Continuous Monitoring
Acme provides 24/7 cybersecurity monitoring.
Capabilities include:
threat detection,
security alerts,
network monitoring,
monthly reporting.
4. FAR Clause Compliance
FAR 52.204-21
Acme will safeguard contractor information systems by:
restricting access,
implementing user authentication,
monitoring system activity.
FAR 52.204-25
Acme certifies that prohibited telecommunications equipment will not be used in performance of this contract.
FAR 52.222-50
Acme maintains employee ethics policies and prohibits trafficking in persons.
FAR 52.245-1
Acme maintains inventory procedures for government property.
5. DFARS Compliance
DFARS 252.204-7012
Acme maintains cybersecurity incident response procedures.
Cyber incidents will be reported to the Government promptly after discovery.
DFARS 252.204-7019
Acme maintains cybersecurity practices aligned with NIST SP 800-171 requirements.
DFARS 252.204-7020
Acme performs periodic cybersecurity reviews.
Assessment documentation can be provided upon request.
6. Staffing
Project Manager:
Sarah Johnson
Cybersecurity Engineers:
6 personnel
Security Analysts:
10 personnel
7. Risk Management
Acme manages risks through:
weekly meetings,
issue tracking,
escalation procedures.
8. Cost Proposal
Estimated Contract Value:
$5,200,000
9. Compliance Matrix
RequirementCompliance Status
FAR 52.204-21Compliant
FAR 52.204-25Compliant
FAR 52.222-50Compliant
FAR 52.245-1Compliant
DFARS 252.204-7012Partial
DFARS 252.204-7019Partial
DFARS 252.204-7020Partial
END OF PROPOSAL

canonical_clause_texts.canonical_text:
Basic Safeguarding of Covered Contractor Information Systems (Nov 2021)

(a) Definitions. As used in this clause—

Covered contractor information system means an information system that is owned or operated by a contractor that processes, stores, or transmits Federal contract information.

Federal contract information means information, not intended for public release, that is provided by or generated for the Government under a contract to develop or deliver a product or service to the Government, but not including information provided by the Government to the public (such as on public websites) or simple transactional information, such as necessary to process payments.

Information means any communication or representation of knowledge such as facts, data, or opinions, in any medium or form, including textual, numerical, graphic, cartographic, narrative, or audiovisual (Committee on National Security Systems Instruction (CNSSI) 4009).

Information system means a discrete set of information resources organized for the collection, processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination, or disposition of information (44 U.S.C. 3502).

Safeguarding means measures or controls that are prescribed to protect information systems.

(b) Safeguarding requirements and procedures.

(1) The Contractor shall apply the following basic safeguarding requirements and procedures to protect covered contractor information systems. Requirements and procedures for basic safeguarding of covered contractor information systems shall include, at a minimum, the following security controls:

(i) Limit information system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, or devices (including other information systems).

(ii) Limit information system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

(iii) Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external information systems.

(iv) Control information posted or processed on publicly accessible information systems.

(v) Identify information system users, processes acting on behalf of users, or devices.

(vi) Authenticate (or verify) the identities of those users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational information systems.

(vii) Sanitize or destroy information system media containing Federal Contract Information before disposal or release for reuse.

(viii) Limit physical access to organizational information systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

(ix) Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity; maintain audit logs of physical access; and control and manage physical access devices.

(x) Monitor, control, and protect organizational communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational information systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of the information systems.

(xi) Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

(xii) Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws in a timely manner.

(xiii) Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organizational information systems.

(xiv) Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.

(xv) Perform periodic scans of the information system and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.

(2) Other requirements. This clause does not relieve the Contractor of any other specific safeguarding requirements specified by Federal agencies and departments relating to covered contractor information systems generally or other Federal safeguarding requirements for controlled unclassified information (CUI) as established by Executive Order 13556.

(c) Subcontracts. The Contractor shall include the substance of this clause, including this paragraph (c), in subcontracts under this contract (including subcontracts for the acquisition of commercial products or commercial services, other than commercially available off-the-shelf items), in which the subcontractor may have Federal contract information residing in or transiting through its information system.

(End of clause)
